aliases: United States Air Force Reserve Command
description: United States Air Force major command responsible for the federal air reserve component
description_wiki: The United States Air Force Reserve (USAFR, AFR, or AFRES) is one of the United States Air Force's (USAF) two air reserve components, alongside the Air National Guard (ANG).

aliases: Amateur Sports Capital of the World
description: capital city of the U.S. state of Indiana and the seat of Marion County
description_wiki: Indianapolis ( , IN-dee-ə-NAP-ə-lis), colloquially known as Indy, is the capital and most populous city of the U.S. state of Indiana and the county seat of Marion County.

description: human settlement in Bexar County, Texas, United States of America
description_wiki: Lackland Air Force Base  (IATA: SKF, ICAO: KSKF, FAA LID: SKF) is a United States Air Force (USAF) base located in Bexar County, Texas, United States.

description: armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia between North Vietnam and South Vietnam, from 1955 to 1975
description_wiki: The Vietnam War (1 November 1955 – 30 April 1975) was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam (Democratic Republic of Vietnam) and South Vietnam (Republic of Vietnam) and their allies.

description: US Air Force base near Wichita Falls, Texas, United States
description_wiki: Sheppard Air Force Base (IATA: SPS, ICAO: KSPS, FAA LID: SPS) is a United States Air Force (USAF) base located five miles (8.0 km) north of the central business district of Wichita Falls, in Wichita County, Texas, United States.

description: city in and county seat of Wichita County, Texas, United States
description_wiki: Wichita Falls ( WITCH-ih-tah) is a city in and the county seat of Wichita County, Texas, United States.

description: public research university in West Lafayette, Indiana, United States
description_wiki: Purdue University is a public land-grant research university in West Lafayette, Indiana, United States, and the flagship campus of the Purdue University system, which also includes Purdue University in Indianapolis as an ongoing expansion of the West Lafayette campus.

aliases: Secretary of Defense of the United States
Secretary of War of the United States
description: head of the U.S. Department of Defense
description_wiki: The United States secretary of defense (SecDef), secondarily titled the secretary of war (SecWar), is the head of the United States Department of Defense (DoD), the executive department of the U.S. Armed Forces, and is a high-ranking member of the cabinet of the United States.

description: former US Air Force base near Columbus, Indiana
description_wiki: Bakalar Air Force Base is a former U.S. Air Force base located 4.4 miles (7.1 km) northeast of Columbus, Indiana.

aliases: Northwest Training Center of the Army Air Corps
description: former U.S. Air Force base located in Ohio

aliases: The Biggest Small Town in America
description: capital city of Ohio, United States of America
description_wiki: Columbus ( kə-LUM-bəs) is the capital and most populous city of the U.S. state of Ohio.

description: U.S. military base
description_wiki: Rickenbacker Air National Guard Base is an Ohio Air National Guard installation at Rickenbacker International Airport near Lockbourne in southern Franklin County.

description: military unit
description_wiki: The 71st Special Operations Squadron is part of the 58th Special Operations Wing at Kirtland Air Force Base, New Mexico.

description: attack aircraft series by Douglas
1947 airlifter family
description_wiki: The Fairchild C-119 Flying Boxcar (Navy and Marine Corps designation R4Q) is an American military transport aircraft developed from the World War II-era Fairchild C-82 Packet, designed to carry cargo, personnel, litter patients, and mechanized equipment, and to drop cargo and troops by parachute.

description: airlifter family
description_wiki: The Lockheed C-141 Starlifter is a retired military strategic airlifter that served with the Military Air Transport Service (MATS), its successor organization the Military Airlift Command (MAC), and finally the Air Mobility Command (AMC) of the United States Air Force (USAF).

description: city of Vietnam, capital of the Khánh Hòa Province
description_wiki: Nha Trang (English:  or ; Vietnamese: [ɲaː1 t͡ɕaːŋ1] ) is a coastal city and the former capital of Khánh Hòa Province, on the South Central Coast of Vietnam.

description: Several posts in the US government
description_wiki: Assistant Secretary of Defense is a title used for many high-level executive positions in the Office of the Secretary of Defense within the U.S. Department of Defense.

description: secured forward military position
description_wiki: A forward operating base (FOB) is any secured forward operational level military position, commonly a military base, that is used to support strategic goals and tactical objectives.

description: airport in Vietnam
description_wiki: Phan Rang Air Base (also called Thành Sơn Air Base) is a Vietnam Air Defence - Air Force (VADAF) (Khong Quan Nhan Dan Viet Nam) military airfield in Vietnam.

description: largest city in Vietnam
description_wiki: Ho Chi Minh City (HCMC; Vietnamese: Thành phố Hồ Chí Minh, IPA: [tʰaɲ˨˩ fow˦˥ ho˨˩ cɪj˦˥ mɨn˧˧] ), also known as Saigon, is the most populous municipality of Vietnam, with a population of more than 14 million in 2025.

description: 1944 landing operations of the Allied invasion of Normandy
description_wiki: The Normandy landings were the landing operations and associated airborne operations on 6 June 1944 of the Allied invasion of Normandy in Operation Overlord during the Second World War.
